headlines.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo could top 20,000 cases in worst case, CDC says
headlines.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda could lead to more than 20,000 cases and over 2,000 deaths in a worst-case scenario, according to a CDC report.

headlines.title: Measles is still spreading in the U.S., with more than 2,000 cases this year
headlines.description: Measles cases in the U.S. reached 2,030 on Friday, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention reported.

headlines.title: GLP-1 overdoses increase at an alarming rate as weight loss drugs become easier to buy online
headlines.description: Within 24 hours of injecting the first dose of a weight loss medication she received following a visit with a telehealth doctor, Karleigh McClain was admitted to the hospital, she said

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: Former NYPD detectives speak out about Luigi Mangione manhunt for first time
headlines.description: Two former NYPD detectives speak exclusively with Lester Holt about those frantic days after the murder of the United Healthcare CEO in broad daylight in Midtown Manhattan. They describe how they ultimately identified him in a Dateline NBC special airing tonight.

headlines.title: Measles cases on the rise since the start of the year
headlines.description: New data from the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention shows more than 2,000 measles cases among unvaccinated people since the start of the year. Public health officials have sounded the alarm over the sharp rise in cases ahead of the World Cup travel influx.

headlines.title: A to Zzzz Guide for Getting Better Sleep in Summer
headlines.description: According to the American Academy of Sleep Medicine, one in three people say they sleep less in the summer so Dr. Wendy Troxel, sleep expert and author of “Sharing the Covers,” joins TODAY to share her solutions for getting well-deserved rest in the hot months. She breaks down the reasons people have more trouble sleeping in the summer like longer days and warmer weather, recommends breathable bedding for hot temperatures, and more.

headlines.title: Boost Your Gut-Brain Connection With These Smart Strategies
headlines.description: Almost 40% of Americans say their daily lives are affected by their bowel habits so Dr. Trisha Pasricha, author and a director of gut-brain research, joins TODAY to break down why gut health is so important. She explains how your gut is responsible for regulating metabolism, immune health, and mood, highlights signs to know if your gut is healthy, and shares ways to improve gut health like eating more fiber.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.

headlines.title: I’ve tested dozens of workout headphones: These are the best
headlines.description: I tested over a dozen workout headphones from brands like Apple, Beats, and Shokz to find our top picks for running, biking, the gym and more.
